title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 2 LIVE: Both Houses adjourned till noon amid uproar; Kharge raises CJP protest crackdown in RS
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
• outside parliament
    July 21, 2026 12:37
    Why is the Prime Minister silent?: Rahul Gandhi on 'brutality' on students

    Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi on Tuesday (July 21, 2026) questioned why the Prime Minister is silent over the police crackdown during the students’ protest march led by CJP in the national capital on Monday (July 20). Mr. Gandhi also met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla earlier today, seeking a discussion in the House over the “brutality” on students.

    “Modi ji has not even apologised for what happened yesterday. The youth are protesting against the education and testing system. The whole country knows that the education and testing system is hollowed out by termites. Why is the Prime Minister silent? He should apologise to the students and stop this nonsense of thrashing students,” he told reporters.

  • outside parliament
    July 21, 2026 12:19
    WATCH | AAP MPs in Rajya Sabha protest against police action on CJP protest

    AAP MPs in Rajya Sabha, including Sanjay Singh, were seen outside the Parliament premises raising slogans and protesting against the police crackdown during the Cockroach Janta Party-led protest march in the national capital on Monday (July 20, 2026).

  • lok sabha
    July 21, 2026 12:16
    Lok Sabha adjourned till 2 p.m.

    “Full country is watching the House proceedings. I request you to take your seats and let the proceedings continue. Is this behaviour appropriate?” Chairperson Sandhya Ray said before adjourning the Lok Sabha till 2 p.m., minutes after the session restarted.

  • rajya sabha
    July 21, 2026 12:11
    Rajya Sabha adjourned till 2 p.m.

    Rajya Sabha has been adjourned to meet again at 2 p.m. shortly after reconvening at 12 p.m.

    ​

    ​

  • lok sabha
    July 21, 2026 12:03
    Lok Sabha restarts proceedings

    The Lok Sabha reconvened at 12 p.m. even as uproar and sloganeering continued in the House. 

    In the Chair: Sandhya Ray

  • July 21, 2026 11:44
    Parliament must have detailed discussion on brutality on students: Rahul Gandhi

    Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi, after meeting Speaker Om Birla, said the Parliament must have a “detailed discussion on the brutality unleashed on students yesterday and on the government’s complete lack of accountability for the examination crisis.”

    He said the Opposition will not the issue be buried and the students’ “voice will be heard on the streets, and in Parliament.”

  • July 21, 2026 11:31
    Opposition MPs, led by Rahul Gandhi, met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla

    As the House adjourned till noon amid uproar, Opposition MPs, led by Lok Sabha LoP Rahul Gandhi, met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla.

  • outside parliament
    July 21, 2026 11:26
    PM Modi stressed on need to punish the guilty: Rijiju on NDA Parliamentary meet

    Union Minister Kiren Rijiju, briefing the media after the NDA Parliamentary Party meet on Tuesday morning (July 21, 2026), said that PM Modi told MPs that the government took immediate action after the leak of NEET exam papers. 

    “Furthermore, prioritising the students’ futures, a re-examination was successfully conducted without delay in declaring the results... He also outlined the stringent steps being taken to avert future leaks... He stressed the need to punish the guilty and collaboratively establish a foolproof system,” Mr. Rijiju said.

    - THB

  • rajya sabha
    July 21, 2026 11:17
    Students' issues need to be heard: Kharge launches attack

    “Our students, who are raising their voice on paper leaks and NEET examination, were attacked, with lathi charge and tear gas shelling. They are in hospitals. Their issues need to be heard,” Mr. Kharge said, as the House broke into a din.

    The Upper House was adjourned till noon.

  • rajya sabha
    July 21, 2026 11:14
    Rajya Sabha adjourned till noon; Kharge raises CJP protest crackdown issue

    The Rajya Sabha was also adjourned till noon shortly after the House began proceedings for the day. The uproar ensued soon after Leader of the House Mallikarjun Kharge raised the issue of the brutal police crackdown on the Cockroach Janta Party-led protest on Monday (July 20, 2026).

    Several hurt in crackdown on CJP Parliament march

    Government crackdown on Cockroach Janta Party protest in Delhi leaves many injured amid demands for Education Minister's resignation.

  • lok sabha
    July 21, 2026 11:07
    Lok Sabha adjourned till noon amid uproar

    Minutes after Lok Sabha began proceedings for the day, the Lower House was adjourned till noon by Speaker Om Birla over uproar and sloganeering by members.

    “Members will get a chance to discussion the issues raised,” Mr. Birla said.

  • July 21, 2026 10:56
    INDI Alliance bloc meeting ahead of the day's Parliament session

    Congress Parliamentary Party chairperson Sonia Gandhi, Leader of the Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi, NCP (SP) MP Supriya Sule and others seen during the INDI Alliance bloc meeting ahead of day of the Monsoon session of Parliament. Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge was also present.

    Photo credit: Special arrangement

    WhatsApp Image 2026-07-21 at 10.51.41 (1).jpeg

    WhatsApp Image 2026-07-21 at 10.51.42.jpeg

  • July 21, 2026 10:46
    Watch | PM Modi at NDA Parliamentary Party Meeting

    The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) Parliamentary Party’s weekly strategy meeting, now known as Mangal Milan, took place ahead of the session at the Parliament House today. 

    During the meeting, Prime Minister Modi is expected to address the leaders as NDA alliance partners also pass a congratulatory motion, honouring Mr. Modi for becoming the longest-serving continuously elected Prime Minister of India. 

    The meetings are traditionally held on Tuesdays during Parliament sessions to demonstrate complete alliance unity.

  • July 21, 2026 10:42
    Congress MP Renuka Chowdhury files adjournment motion in RS

    Congress MP Renuka Chowdhury on Tuesday (July 21, 2026) moved an adjournment motion under Rule 267 in Rajya Sabha, alleging that several gates of Parliament were sealed and internet services were suspended, preventing MPs from leaving the House to meet citizens protesting nearby. Heavy deployment of armed personnel and barricades created an “unprecedented lockdown of Parliament”, she wrote.

  • July 21, 2026 10:42
    Rajya Sabha agenda for the day
    1. Question Hour
    2. Consideration of any business entered in the List of Business for Monday (July 20, 2026) and not concluded on that day.
    3. Motion of election of a member to AIIMS, Bhubaneswar
  • July 21, 2026 10:42
    List of business in Lok Sabha on July 21
    1. Question Hour

    2. Consideration of any item of Government Business entered in the List of Business for Monday (July 20, 2026) and not concluded on that day. 

Published - July 21, 2026 10:39 am IST

title: Farmers stopped at Punjab-Haryana boundary en route to Delhi rally against India-U.S. trade deal
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Punjab-based farmers under the banner of the Desh Bachao Morcha (DBM)were on Tuesday (July 21, 2026) stopped by the police at Shambhu-Ambala and Khanauri-Jind, the inter-State boundaries between Haryana and Punjab, as they headed to Delhi to participate in rally against the the proposed India-U.S. trade deal.

Several farmers from Haryana began moving on foot towards Delhi after their vehicles and buses were stopped by the police at different places.

Multi-layer barricades have been installed by the Haryana police at the inter-State boundaries as farmers attempted to enter Haryana in their vehicles and buses. The Ambala police has issued a traffic advisory, asking people to take alternate roads as the Shambhu-Ambala road has been closed at the boundary.

Farmer leader and coordinator of DBM, Sarwan Singh Pandher, said that farmers want to peacefully go to Delhi and participate in the rally, but the BJP-led Haryana government has stopped farmers by erecting barricades. “We are ready to talk to the local administration and are in no mood for confrontation,” he said.

Meanwhile, Bharatiya Kisan Union (Charuni) president Gurnam Singh Charuni was allegedly detained by police in Kurukshetra in Haryana on July 20 as he headed to Delhi for the rally. BKU (Charuni) spokesperson Prince Waraich told media persons that Mr. Charuni’s vehicle was intercepted by police near Pehowa. BKU (Charuni) is a member outfit of the DBM.

“We urge the Central government to prioritise the interests of farmers, workers, small traders, small industries and India’s economic and food sovereignty and refrain from implementing any agreement that goes against the interests of the people,” he said.

“If our concerns continue to be ignored and the agreement is pushed forward, the movement will be intensified,” he said.

Farmers associated with DBM, an umbrella body of farmer unions from different States, had announced a rally in New Delhi on Tuesday (July 21, 2026). The DBM noted that the deal is not limited merely to the import and export of a few products; instead, it may cover several crucial sectors, including agriculture, dairy, industry, digital trade, services, intellectual property rights, energy, investment and market access.

Published - July 21, 2026 10:06 am IST

title: Odessa ship attack: Bodies of two Indian seafarers identified
author: M. Kalyanaraman
content_html:
Two Indian seafarers have been identified among at least ten crew members killed when the general cargo vessel MV Golden Leo was struck by missiles while departing the Port of Odesa on Sunday (July 19, 2026). Two among the dead Indians dead have been identified as Chief Officer Akhil Jovan, 26, of Kerala, and Ordinary Seaman Abhishek Nishad, 21, of Uttar Pradesh. Both had signed on to the vessel at Istanbul on June 12.

The Guinea-Bissau-flagged vessel, carrying a cargo of grain, came under attack at approximately 1600 hrs Ukraine local time as it exited the port. Three missiles struck the ship, according to official sources. A Ukrainian port pilot was on board at the time, guiding the vessel out of harbour.

Golden Leo was carrying 17 crew members — five Indian nationals and 12 Syrian nationals — besides the Ukrainian pilot. The ship was under the command of Master Ahmad Hamwia, a Syrian national.

All ten crew initially listed as missing have now been confirmed dead, and all bodies recovered. Eight of the ten remain unidentified, owing to severe burns sustained in the attack. Identification is pending through established forensic procedure. The bodies of two more Indian seafarers is feared to be among them.

Eight others, including a third Indian national serving as Third Officer, were rescued and are undergoing hospital treatment.

The Indian seafarers on board were engaged through a Navi Mumbai-based recruitment and placement service licence (RPSL) holder.

Golden Leo, built and operating under the Guinea-Bissau flag, was struck just outside the Odesa Sea Port Control area — one of the exit channels vessels use after loading at the port’s grain terminals.

Published - July 21, 2026 11:50 am IST

title: India summons Russian envoy over killing of four Indian seafarers near Odessa, Ukraine
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
India on Tuesday (July 21, 2026) summoned the Russian charge d'affaires (CDA) and lodged a strong protest over the killing of four Indian seafarers aboard a Guinea Bissau-flagged merchant vessel off the coast of Ukraine.

The Ministry of External Affairs, in a press release on Tuesday (July 21, 2026), said that the Russian CDA Vladimir Ladanov was summoned and the Ministry conveyed to him “India’s grave concerns and unequivocal condemnation of the attack on the commercial vessel MV Golden Leo on 19 July 2026, which resulted in the tragic loss of four Indian lives”.

The Ministry said that such attacks undermine the safety, security, and stability of international maritime commerce.

“The Russian Chargé d’Affaires was requested to convey India’s strong concerns to his authorities that the targeting of commercial shipping and the resulting loss of innocent civilian lives are unacceptable and must be avoided,” the Ministry said.

Russia attacked the civilian vessel navigating Ukraine's maritime corridor on Sunday (July 19, 2026) and its crew included citizens of Syria and India. The Guinea-Bissau-flagged ship was hit by Russian cruise missiles, the Ukrainian Air Force said. The vessel— MV Golden Leo— had 17 crew members, including five Indian nationals, according to the MEA.

Published - July 21, 2026 11:15 am IST

title: From IAC to paper leaks: Why anti-system movements pose a unique political challenge
author: Sandeep Phukan
content_html:
The story so far: The student-led protests over recurring examination paper leaks and the conduct of the NEET examination have inevitably drawn comparisons with the India Against Corruption (IAC) movement that rattled the second United Progressive Alliance (UPA) government between 2011 and 2013.

The immediate triggers are different: corruption under the UPA and the credibility of public examinations under the National Democratic Alliance (NDA). Yet both movements display the potential to disrupt politics by presenting an outsider’s challenge to the political establishment.

Published - July 21, 2026 08:54 am IST

title: 5,289 cyber awareness programmes held across Gujarat
author: Abhinay Deshpande
content_html:
The Gujarat police has organised 5,289 cyber safety awareness programmes across the State in the first two weeks of its 28-day special campaign, Operation Surakshit Cyberspace, aimed at ensuring the safe use of online platforms by women and children.

The campaign focuses on preventing cybercrime through community engagement, technology-driven surveillance, victim outreach, cyber awareness and prompt legal action.

Additional Director General of Police (CID Crime Women Cell) Ajay Chaudhary said ensuring the online safety of women and children was a priority in the digital era. “Through Operation Surakshit Cyberspace, Gujarat Police is creating a safer digital environment by raising awareness about cyber threats such as deepfakes and sextortion. Gujarat Police remains fully committed to the safety and security of every citizen,” he said.

According to the police, 1,874 awareness and outreach programmes were organised during the first week, followed by 3,415 special sessions in the second week focusing on online threats such as sextortion, deepfakes and cyberstalking. The programmes reached lakhs of citizens across the State.

During the first week, the campaign included 185 launch events on digital and physical safety, 438 Cyber Safety Day programmes in schools, and 336 awareness activities under Kishori Diwas for adolescent girls. The police also conducted 318 programmes for parents, 281 training sessions for teachers, 207 awareness programmes in colleges and organised 109 cyber safety rallies across districts.

In the second week, Gujarat Police organised 4,172 thematic programmes and public awareness camps. These included 743 programmes on social media safety, 578 deepfake awareness sessions, 514 cyberstalking prevention programmes and 520 events under Anti-Sextortion Day.

The police also conducted 512 gaming safety programmes, 466 online grooming prevention sessions and 461 public awareness camps in remote areas.

As part of the campaign, responsibilities have been assigned to field officers ranging from police constables and head constables to assistant sub-inspectors, police sub-inspectors and police inspectors. Their performance is being assessed on five parameters: awareness, prevention, enforcement, quality of investigation and public trust.

They appealed to citizens to report cybercrime or online harassment immediately by calling the National Cyber Helpline at 1930 and registering a complaint.

Published - July 21, 2026 11:09 am IST
